taxonomy: Eremolepis punctulata is an extinct species of fish. Its classification is within the family Eremolepidae and the class Sarcopterygii. The Sarcopterygii class is further divided into the infraclass Tetrapodomorpha and the superclass Sarcopterygii. Eremolepis punctulata is considered to be one of the earliest known tetrapods. It is classified as an ancient fish with characteristics of the first land-dwelling vertebrates.
